judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are strong and nearly identical on (a), (b), and (c), but Report 2 additionally recommends dropping the hardcoded webhook count in favor of pointing at the source code (expectation d), while Report 1 only updates the count to 5 without addressing the drift-prone nature of hardcoding it, making Report 2 the more complete answer.

report_1.evidence: So a new refund(tenant_id, amount_cents, ...) accessor belongs in gateway.py... invoices.py:4,14-16 — imports chargekit directly... dunning.py:2,9-13 — imports chargekit directly... The only file that legitimately deviates by the doc's own definition is medibill/backend/app/billing/errors.py:6-9, which imports chargekit.errors for exception re-export — this is explicitly called out as the documented exception

report_2.evidence: New refund calls should live in medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py... invoices.py:4 and :14 — imports chargekit directly... dunning.py:2 and :9 — same pattern... The only sanctioned exception is medibill/backend/app/billing/errors.py:6, which imports just the SDK's error classes... this one does match what the doc names as the exception

report_1.evidence: InvoiceOut (medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22) has six fields, not five: id, tenant_id, amount_cents, currency, status, and external_ref. external_ref was added by migration 0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql and commit c23da88 (MEDB-214)

report_2.evidence: InvoiceOut (medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22) now has a sixth field, external_ref, added in commit c23da88 (MEDB-214) together with migration medibill/backend/migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql:1

report_1.evidence: It should be corrected before, or as part of, the refund feature work — not deferred further. ... Treat the doc update as part of the same PR/ticket that adds refund support, not a follow-up

report_2.evidence: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md should be corrected now, before the refund feature is built on top of it ... it should be fixed *before* any refund-related PR lands, not folded silently into the refund PR as an incidental change... This correction should have happened in the same commit as c23da88 (MEDB-214)

the fix isn't just to bump "4" to "5" (that number will drift again the next time someone adds an event type, e.g. refund_failed for the upcoming refund feature). I'd change the doc to either drop the count and list the handled types by pointing at the if kind == ... chain in webhooks.py as the source of truth
